Racmacs 1.2.9
  • Reference
  • Articles
  • Changelog

Optimize an acmap with custom starting coordinates

Source: R/map_optimize.R
optimizeMapStart.Rd

A variant of optimizeMap() that lets you control how the starting coordinates for each optimisation run are chosen. In standard Racmacs the L-BFGS optimizer starts each run from uniformly random positions; this function lets you supply any alternative — a fixed list of pre-computed coordinates, a generating function, or one of several built-in distributions.

optimizeMapStart(
  map,
  number_of_dimensions,
  number_of_optimizations,
  starting_coords,
  coord_args = list(),
  minimum_column_basis = "none",
  fixed_column_bases = NULL,
  titer_weights = NULL,
  sort_optimizations = TRUE,
  check_convergence = TRUE,
  verbose = TRUE,
  options = list()
)

Arguments

map

The acmap data object

number_of_dimensions

The number of dimensions for the new map

number_of_optimizations

The number of optimization runs to perform

starting_coords

Specifies how starting coordinates are generated. One of three forms:

List

A pre-computed list of length number_of_optimizations. Each element must be a named list with entries ag_coords (n_antigens × n_dimensions matrix) and sr_coords (n_sera × n_dimensions matrix).

Function

A function with signature function(n_ag, n_sr, ndim, D, ...) that returns list(ag_coords, sr_coords). It is called once per optimisation run with the numeric table-distance matrix D (NAs for missing titers) and any additional arguments supplied via coord_args.

Character string

A built-in name ("uniform", "normal", "disk", or "genetic") — see Details — or the suffix of any standard R distribution whose random generator follows the r<name>() naming convention (e.g. "t" for rt(), "cauchy" for rcauchy(), "exp" for rexp()). Parameters are passed via coord_args.

coord_args

A named list of additional arguments forwarded to the generating function or built-in distribution. Ignored when starting_coords is a pre-computed list. Examples: list(table_dist_factor = 3) for "uniform"; list(sd = 2) for "normal".

minimum_column_basis

The minimum column basis to use

fixed_column_bases

A vector of fixed column bases; NA entries are calculated from minimum_column_basis.

titer_weights

An optional matrix of per-titer weights

sort_optimizations

Sort optimizations by stress afterwards?

check_convergence

Check for convergence of the top runs?

verbose

Report progress messages?

options

List of optimizer options; see RacOptimizer.options()

Value

Returns the acmap object updated with new optimizations.

Details

Built-in named options

"uniform"

Uniform random draw from \([\mathit{lo},\, \mathit{hi}]^{\mathrm{ndim}}\). coord_args may contain min and max (defaults: ±max_table_dist). Replicates the standard Racmacs random initialisation.

"normal"

Isotropic Gaussian centred at the origin. coord_args may contain mean (default 0) and sd (default max_table_dist / 2).

"disk"

Uniform random points inside an n-dimensional ball (a circle/disk in 2D, a sphere/ball in 3D). Points are generated by sampling a uniformly random direction on the unit sphere and an independent radius scaled as \(r \cdot U^{1/\mathrm{ndim}}\) so that density is exactly uniform by volume. coord_args may contain r (radius; default max_table_dist).

"genetic"

Classical MDS applied to the pairwise Hamming distances of all antigen and serum amino acid sequences stored in the map (via agSequences() and srSequences()). The MDS coordinates are scaled so that their RMS spread matches the RMS table distance, then small isotropic Gaussian noise is added per run to create diversity. coord_args may contain noise_sd (default max_table_dist * 0.05). Sequences must be set on the map before calling this function.

Any standard R distribution

For any distribution with an r<name>() function in the search path, pass the suffix as the string. All coord_args entries are forwarded as named arguments. No data-informed scale defaults are applied — choose parameters appropriate for your map's antigenic unit scale.

# Student-t (heavier tails than normal)
optimizeMapStart(map, 2, 100, starting_coords = "t",
                 coord_args = list(df = 5))

# Cauchy (very heavy tails)
optimizeMapStart(map, 2, 100, starting_coords = "cauchy",
                 coord_args = list(location = 0, scale = 3))

# Exponential (one-sided; may be useful with centring)
optimizeMapStart(map, 2, 100, starting_coords = "exp",
                 coord_args = list(rate = 0.2))

Using custom functions

The function interface is designed to match the five starting-coordinate algorithms in a companion analysis script. Any function that accepts (n_ag, n_sr, ndim, D) and returns a named list with ag_coords and sr_coords can be supplied directly:

optimizeMapStart(
  map, 2, 100,
  starting_coords = start_lhs   # Latin Hypercube
)

optimizeMapStart(
  map, 2, 100,
  starting_coords = start_cmds_perturbed,
  coord_args = list(noise_sd = 1.5)
)

Dimensional annealing

starting_coords is incompatible with options = list(dim_annealing = TRUE) because dimensional annealing starts in 5D and requires random initialisation in that higher space. A warning is issued and dimensional annealing is disabled when both are supplied.

See also

optimizeMap() for the standard (random) optimisation. relaxMap() to re-relax existing coordinates.

Other map optimization functions: RacOptimizer.options(), make.acmap(), moveTrappedPoints(), optimizeMap(), randomizeCoords(), relaxMap(), relaxMapOneStep()
